title: DrawHunt
summary: A draw-and-guess party game for the browser - one player sketches the secret word while everyone else races to guess it.
description: DrawHunt is a browser-based take on the classic draw-and-guess game. One player gets a word and sketches it on a shared canvas while the others type their guesses against the clock.
highlights: Real-time canvas drawing and guessing loop

title: Super Seven Cards
summary: A digital implementation of the Sevens card game (a.k.a. Card Dominoes), built in Python.
description: A Python implementation of Sevens / Card Dominoes, where players build sequences outward from the sevens. Includes the full game logic and turn handling.
highlights: Complete Sevens game rules and turn engine

title: Cricket Analysis & Prediction
summary: Exploratory data analysis and predictive modelling on cricket match data.
description: A data-science notebook that explores historical cricket data and builds models to predict innings scores and outcomes - feature engineering, EDA, and regression in one place.

title: Line Encoder & Scrambler
summary: Digital line-encoding schemes and scrambling techniques, implemented from scratch in Python.
description: An implementation of classic digital line-encoding schemes - NRZ-L, NRZ-I, RZ, Manchester, Differential Manchester, AMI, and pseudoternary - plus advanced scrambling techniques B8ZS and 4B5B.
highlights: Seven line-encoding schemes implemented from scratch

title: OCR String Similarity
summary: Image-similarity and fuzzy string-matching experiments for cleaning up noisy OCR output.
description: A set of experiments in image similarity and fuzzy string matching aimed at de-noising and reconciling OCR output against references - the same class of problem behind my day-job parser work.
highlights: Image similarity matching for OCR de-duplication
Fuzzy string comparison for noisy text

title: Salary Predictions
summary: A regression model that predicts software-developer salaries from experience and skills.
description: A machine-learning project that predicts software-developer salaries from features like experience and skill set, wrapped in a simple, explainable regression model.
